desc: A unique 10-character identifier assigned to the reporting financial institution.
A code indicating the specific federal agency with supervisory authority over the reporting institution (e.g., OCC, FRS, FDIC, CFPB).
Indicates whether the loan record failed any internal validity or quality edits during the data processing phase.
The Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) or Metropolitan Division (MD) where the property securing the loan is geographically located.
A standardized two-digit FIPS code identifying the US state where the property is located.
A standardized three-digit FIPS code identifying the county where the property is located.
The specific census tract number where the property is located, used to evaluate neighborhood-level demographic and economic patterns.
A unique sequence number internally generated by the responding institution to uniquely identify each individual loan or application record.
The specific type of loan being applied for or originated, categorizing whether it is a Conventional loan or a government-backed loan (e.g., FHA, VA, FSA/RHS).
The structural classification of the property securing the loan, such as a one-to-four family dwelling, manufactured housing, or a multifamily building.
The primary intent of the borrower for taking out the loan, categorized mainly into home purchase, home improvement, or refinancing an existing debt.
Indicates the borrower's intended residency status for the property, specifying whether it will be occupied as their principal dwelling or serve as an investment/second home.
The total requested or originated dollar amount of the loan, aggregated and reported in thousands of dollars.
Specifies whether the applicant requested a formal preapproval program assessment before identifying a specific property to purchase.
The final disposition or outcome of the application, detailing if the loan was originated, denied, withdrawn by the applicant, or approved but ultimately not accepted.
The self-reported or observed ethnic background of the primary applicant (e.g., Hispanic/Latino or Not Hispanic/Latino).
The primary racial category identified by the primary applicant (e.g., White, Asian, Black or African American, American Indian).
The self-reported or observed biological sex of the primary applicant.
The gross annual income that the financial institution relied upon when evaluating the applicant's creditworthiness, reported in thousands of dollars.
Identifies the type of institutional entity (e.g., Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, a commercial bank) that purchased the loan on the secondary market within the reporting year.
The primary determining factor for denying the loan application, such as an excessively high debt-to-income ratio, poor credit history, or insufficient collateral.
A flag indicating whether the loan meets the high-cost thresholds making it subject to the Home Ownership and Equity Protection Act (HOEPA).
The lien priority status of the loan, indicating whether the debt is secured by a first lien, a subordinate (second) lien, or remains unsecured.
A technical flag used to determine if the application was received before or after January 1, 2004, which affects certain reporting compliance rules.
The total estimated number of residents living within the property's specific census tract.
The percentage of the total population within the census tract that identifies with a minority race or ethnicity.
The annual median family income for the broader Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) or division, as estimated and adjusted annually by the FFIEC.
A comparative percentage ratio that expresses the specific census tract's median family income relative to the broader MSA/MD's median family income.
The total count of residential dwellings within the census tract that are actively lived in by their owners rather than renters.
The total count of residential buildings in the census tract that are structurally designed to house fewer than five independent families.
